title: OverlayView struct and init lack doc comments
detail: The struct itself has no /// doc comment explaining its purpose (full-screen overlay for break reminders). The init parameters (type, duration, hapticsEnabled, onAnalyticsEvent, onDismiss) also lack per-parameter documentation. Every other major view and service in the codebase has top-level documentation. This is the most user-visible view in the app.

title: Eight extracted subviews have no doc comments
detail: backgroundGradient, dismissButton, centerContent, iconAura, headlineSection, countdownRing, actionSection, and swipeUpDismissGesture are all undocumented. The MARK section header exists but individual computed properties have zero /// comments. Names are self-documenting so this is P2, not P1 — but the rest of the codebase documents subviews when they exist.

title: withMotionSafe() not used in OverlayView despite being created for this pattern
detail: OverlayView has three manual `if reduceMotion { ... } else { withAnimation { ... } }` blocks (lines 215, 239, 263). The withMotionSafe() helper in Components.swift was created to eliminate exactly this pattern. SettingsView already uses it. OverlayView should adopt it for consistency. Note: the dismiss paths set multiple properties plus dispatch async, so withMotionSafe may need the closure to encompass only the animation portion — verify before applying.
